string licenseKeyFormatting(string s, int k) {

   // process s to cleanStr

   string cleanStr;

   for (char c : s) {

      if (c != '-') {

         cleanStr.push_back(toupper(c));

      }

   }

   int len = cleanStr.size();

   if (len ==0) return ""; // but according to problem's constraints, s has at least one character, but maybe all dashes. However, if s is all dashes, then cleanStr is empty. But in that case, return empty string.

   // compute firstGroupLength

   int firstGroupLength = len %k;

   if (firstGroupLength ==0) {

      firstGroupLength =k;

   }

   // split into groups

   vector<string> groups;

   groups.push_back( cleanStr.substr(0, firstGroupLength) );

   int pos = firstGroupLength;

   while (pos < len) {

      groups.push_back( cleanStr.substr(pos, k) );

      pos +=k;

   }

   // join with '-'

   string res;

   for (int i=0; i < groups.size(); ++i) {

      if (i !=0) res += '-';

      res += groups[i];

   }

   return res;

}

string licenseKeyFormatting(string s, int k) {
    string cleanStr;
    for (char c : s) {
        if (c != '-') {
            cleanStr.push_back(toupper(c));
        }
    }
    int len = cleanStr.size();
    if (len == 0) return "";

        // Wait, original code:
        firstGroupLength = len % k;
        if (firstGroupLength == 0) {
            firstGroupLength = k;
        }
    }
    // So code:
    int firstGroupLength = len % k;
    if (firstGroupLength == 0) {
        firstGroupLength = k;
    }
    vector<string> groups;
    groups.push_back(cleanStr.substr(0, firstGroupLength));
    int pos = firstGroupLength;
    while (pos < len) {
        groups.push_back(cleanStr.substr(pos, k));
        pos += k;
    }
    string res;
    for (int i = 0; i < groups.size(); ++i) {
        if (i != 0) res += '-';
        res += groups[i];
    }
    return res;
}

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    string licenseKeyFormatting(string s, int k) {
        string cleanStr;
        for (char c : s) {
            if (c != '-') {
                cleanStr.push_back(toupper(c));
            }
        }
        int len = cleanStr.size();
        if (len == 0) return "";
        int firstGroupLength = len % k;
        if (firstGroupLength == 0) {
            firstGroupLength = k;
        }
        vector<string> groups;
        groups.push_back(cleanStr.substr(0, firstGroupLength));
        int pos = firstGroupLength;
        while (pos < len) {
            groups.push_back(cleanStr.substr(pos, k));
            pos += k;
        }
        string res;
        for (int i = 0; i < groups.size(); ++i) {
            if (i != 0) res += '-';
            res += groups[i];
        }
        return res;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution processes the input string to remove dashes and convert to uppercase, then splits the cleaned string into groups with the first group of length `len % k` (or `k` if modulo is zero) followed by groups of size `k`. This approach ensures linear time and space complexity, making it efficient for large inputs. The main strength is its simplicity and linearity, while a potential weakness is the use of additional space for intermediate strings.
